misconfig_patterns: OSS coordinator default backend = MQTT_S3 pointing at TensorOpera's hosted broker — operators reusing the shipped example federate THROUGH TensorOpera by default
Self-hosted Mosquitto/EMQX is a deliberate substitution; example mqtt_config.yaml default auth state unconfirmed in docs
No first-party self-hosted dashboard — 'on-premise' means TensorOpera control plane + on-prem workers bound via fedml login
Anonymous MQTT CONNECT on self-hosted broker = real-time read of FedML topic tree (gradients in transit)

active_probe.false_positive_check: Require fedml-pattern topic tree (fedml_<runId>_<role>_<id>) on subscribe wildcard — generic Mosquitto won't match

deployment_tells: Three populations: research consortia (incl. EU clinical-stroke FL PaaS), edge/IoT (Jetson/Pi/Android), TensorOpera tenants
S3-compatible store is mandatory — world-readable bucket = gradients/checkpoints leak
KubeFATE ships a FedML-agent-on-K8s recipe (outbound-only, no Ingress by default — internal-only signal)
PyTorch Serve / vLLM / Triton for the serving half; FedML has its own tracking (no MLflow/W&B default)
pivot_paths: MQTT anonymous CONNECT + wildcard subscribe -> real-time topic tree enumeration (metadata, read-only)
World-readable S3 bucket discovery -> model checkpoints / gradient dumps
gRPC sendMessage entry (Octopus surface) -> CVE-2026-5536 deserialization (DO NOT EXERCISE)
Android Beehive FileUtils path-traversal -> CVE-2026-5535 (DO NOT EXERCISE)
vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-5535 — Beehive Android FileUtils.java MQTT-message path traversal (FedML <= 0.8.9, public PoC)
No SECURITY.md, no GHSA entries; no documented default creds on OSS
